rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dibromination of terpenes and alkenes using NaBr as bromide source in an undivided cell with glassy carbon electrodes
electrochemical_role: Anodic 2-electron oxidation of bromide to Br2 (in equilibrium with tribromide Br3-); cathodic reduction of water to H2 (and hypobromite formation in undivided cell)
electron_flow: At the anode, 2 Br- is oxidized (2 e-) to Br2, which equilibrates with Br3-; Br2 reacts with the alkene to form a bromonium ion that is opened by Br- to give the 1,2-dibromide. The cathode reduces 2 H2O (2 e-) to H2 and hydroxide, balancing charge.
rate_determining_step: Anodic generation of Br2 from bromide (slow, controlled in-situ release accounts for high selectivity)

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of NaBr
probe_reagents.observation: Two oxidation events observed: Br- to Br2/Br3- equilibrium, then Br3- to Br2

probe_reagents.name: pH monitoring (divided vs undivided cell)
probe_reagents.observation: Cathodic chamber pH rises from 6 to 14 in divided cell (OH-/H2 evolution); undivided cell pH remains constant, consistent with hypobromite formation

probe_reagents.name: X-ray crystallography of 2b
probe_reagents.observation: Confirmed (1R,2R,4S,8S)-tetrabromide diastereomer, supporting stereospecific anti-addition via bromonium intermediates

probe_reagents.name: Br2 control (conventional)
probe_reagents.observation: Br2 gives 22% 2a and 13% 2b (poor selectivity vs 54% / 0% for electrochemical)

probe_reagents.name: DMSO/HBr control
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 39% 2a and 10% 2b; lower selectivity than electrochemical method

notes: Selectivity rationalized by slow, controlled in-situ generation of Br2 at the anode versus bulk Br2 addition. Electron-withdrawing groups on alkene reduce reactivity at that position, directing bromination to more nucleophilic alkenes in polyenes (e.g. terminal alkene of carvone/perillaldehyde). Cathodic lability of C-Br bonds required a divided PTFE cell for tetrabromination of limonene to 2b. Hypobromite is invoked as a side species in undivided cells based on constant pH.
